mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 6% indicates tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in UNRATE decline scenarios are negative or flat (return < 0.5%) despite unemployment rate falling below 6%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through increased consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive growth, or pairwise correlation between proxies drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2%) combined with elevated policy rates (FEDFUNDS > 4%) signals a tightening cycle, causing S&P 500 60-day returns to decline due to higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when both CPIAUCSL > 2% and FEDFUNDS > 4%, or pairwise signal strength drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 100k) indicates expanding employment, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or signal strength falls below 0.2 in 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in residential construction (RSAFS > 450k) reflects strong housing demand, driving S&P 500 5-day returns higher through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when RSAFS exceeds 450k, or the series shows no significant correlation with SPX returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 105) indicates expanding manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through sectoral earnings growth.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when INDPRO exceeds 105, or the series shows no significant positive correlation with SPX (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer demand (CPIAUCSL > 0.3% MoM) during late-cycle expansion drives S&P 500 60-day returns higher through inflationary pressure and sectoral re-rating.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative when CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM, or the signal shows no significant positive correlation with SPX (p > 0.1).
